summary: Verified TESS frontend now targets the Render backend (https://tess-1-rq34.onrender.com) and no code hardcodes localhost/preview API URLs. All 5 backend pytest tests pass: /api/health (has_gemini_key=true, model=gemini-2.5-flash), /api/tess/chat (200 casual reply), /api/extract-metadata (class=6, subject=maths, question=1, academic=true), CORS preflight OPTIONS and actual POST both return ACAO covering the preview origin. Frontend loads Auth screen cleanly with 0 localhost/preview /api requests. Bundle inspection shows only ${BACKEND_URL}/api/tess/chat and ${BACKEND_URL}/api/detect-subject with BACKEND_URL bound to the Render host. A live browser fetch from the preview origin to https://tess-1-rq34.onrender.com/api/detect-subject returned 200 with valid CORS, confirming the browser can reach Render.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/src/api/tess.ts exposes tessChat + detectSubject only. The extract-metadata endpoint exists on Render but is not called from the frontend; if it is meant to be used, add a wrapper in src/api/tess.ts and call from the appropriate screen.
BACKEND_URL from process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL is not runtime-guarded; if the env var were missing the fetch would silently target undefined/api/... Consider adding a boot-time assert.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Render URL = https://tess-1-rq34.onrender.com; preview frontend = https://tess-smart-tutor.preview.emergentagent.com. Backend is on Render free tier — expect occasional cold-start latency and 503 quota errors from Gemini which are the app's expected silent-error path. Test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_render_backend.py can be reused; increase timeout if cold-start.
